title: China-to-Bahrain Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Grid Frequency and Voltage Settings
rows.target_requirement: Bahrain's electricity supply system operates at 50 Hz / 400 V (three-phase) and 230 V (single-phase). Solar PV inverters must be configured for 50 Hz grid frequency and must comply with EWA voltage tolerance requirements: voltage at the point of connection must not vary from rated voltage by more than ±6% under normal operating conditions, and connection or disconnection must not cause voltage variations exceeding 3% at the point of connection. Anti-islanding protection is required to prevent operation when the EWA grid section is de-energised. Inverters designed and pre-set for China's 50 Hz / 220 V / 380 V grid may require re-configuration for Bahrain's 230 V / 400 V nominal system.
target_standards: EWA technical requirements for distributed renewable energy generation (grid voltage and frequency tolerances)
rows.china_equivalent: China's grid operates at 50 Hz, so frequency alignment is not itself a gap. However, China's nominal LV system is 220 V (single-phase) and 380 V (three-phase), while Bahrain uses 230 V / 400 V. Voltage set-point, under/over-voltage, and under/over-frequency trip thresholds must be verified and adjusted against EWA technical requirements before grid connection.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 (grid frequency and voltage settings for China-market inverters)
rows.gap: Verify inverter firmware and hardware voltage range covers 230 V / 400 V nominal. Adjust trip thresholds and anti-islanding settings to EWA tolerances. Document settings in commissioning records for EWA-approved consultant review.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected and net-metering installations; EWA voltage and anti-islanding requirements apply
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for China domestic grid connection under GB/T 19964-2024 and GB/T 37408-2019; thresholds differ from Bahrain

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Bahrain uses 50 Hz / 230 V / 400 V. Inverters pre-set for China's 220 V / 380 V nominal voltages need voltage-range verification and protection-threshold adjustment before EWA grid connection. Document all settings for EWA-approved consultant sign-off.

source.publisher: Electricity and Water Authority (EWA), Kingdom of Bahrain

rows.requirement: BSMD Standards Screening and IEC 62109 Safety Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Bahrain's standards body, the Bahrain Standards and Metrology Directorate (BSMD) within the Ministry of Industry and Commerce (MOIC), adopts Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) standards issued by the GCC Standardization Organization (GSO) or prepares national standards. Bahrain is an IEC associate member and participates in IECEE. For grid-connected solar PV inverters, IEC 62109-1 (safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems, general requirements) and IEC 62109-2 (inverter requirements) represent the international safety baseline expected by EWA-approved consultants, project owners, and insurers. Public sources do not confirm a single mandatory whole-product inverter import certificate, but EWA material-approval listing is the operative gate for net-metering and grid-connected installations.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1:2010 (Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements)
IEC 62109-2:2011 (Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters)
BSMD / GSO standards adoption process (MOIC, Kingdom of Bahrain)
rows.china_equivalent: China solar PV inverter files commonly include GB/T 37408-2019 (technical specifications for photovoltaic grid-connected inverters) and GB/T 19964-2024 (technical requirements for connecting photovoltaic power stations to power systems). These China documents can support technical due diligence but do not replace BSMD standards confirmation, EWA material-approval listing, or a project-owner or insurer request for IEC 62109-numbered test reports.
rows.gap: Confirm the exact Bahrain route by HS code, product configuration, installation type (net-metering, grid-connected, off-grid), and project specification. Obtain IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 test reports from an accredited laboratory. Apply for EWA material-approval listing before supply to net-metering or grid-connected projects. Prepare a clause matrix showing where China GB/T test data maps to IEC 62109 requirements.

mandatory_status.target: EWA material-approval listing is mandatory for inverters used in net-metering and grid-connected installations in Bahrain; IEC 62109 evidence is expected by EWA-approved consultants, project owners, and insurers
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 37408-2019 and GB/T 19964-2024 are recommended standards; compliance may be required by project, grid operator, or contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat BSMD as the standards-screening gate and obtain IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 accredited-lab reports. Apply for EWA material-approval listing before supplying inverters to Bahrain net-metering or grid-connected projects.

source.publisher: Bahrain Standards and Metrology Directorate (BSMD), Ministry of Industry and Commerce

rows.requirement: China GB/T 37408-2019 and GB/T 19964-2024 Baseline vs Bahrain Expectations
rows.target_requirement: Bahrain does not publish a list of Chinese GB/T standards that it formally accepts as equivalent to IEC or GSO standards for solar PV inverters. Project owners, EWA-approved consultants, lenders, and insurers in Bahrain will typically look for IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 accredited test reports and EWA material-approval listing rather than China GB/T documentation.
rows.china_equivalent: GB/T 37408-2019 covers technical specifications for photovoltaic grid-connected inverters for the China market, including safety, EMC, and grid-interaction requirements. GB/T 19964-2024 sets technical requirements for connecting photovoltaic power stations to power systems. Both documents use Chinese language and Chinese grid parameters and are not direct IEC 62109 equivalents.
rows.gap: Prepare a clause-by-clause gap analysis between GB/T 37408-2019 and IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2. Where gaps exist, obtain supplementary accredited-lab testing against IEC 62109. Translate critical documentation into English for EWA and project-owner review. Do not submit China-language GB/T test reports alone as the compliance basis for Bahrain projects.

mandatory_status.target: IEC 62109 evidence mandatory for EWA material-approval listing; GB/T reports alone not accepted
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 37408-2019 is a recommended standard for China domestic market; mandatory requirements depend on product certification route and contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China GB/T 37408-2019 and GB/T 19964-2024 documentation supports internal technical due diligence but is not accepted as a standalone compliance basis for Bahrain's EWA material-approval or project requirements. IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 accredited test reports are required.

source.publisher: Bahrain Standards and Metrology Directorate (BSMD), Ministry of Industry and Commerce

rows.requirement: EWA Grid-Connection and Net-Metering Material Approval
rows.target_requirement: The Electricity and Water Authority (EWA) operates Bahrain's electricity network and administers the net-metering scheme established by Cabinet Resolution No. 2 of 2017. EWA maintains a list of approved solar PV materials, including inverters, through its Solar PV Material-Approval process. Only inverters on the EWA-approved materials list may be used in net-metering and grid-connected installations. EWA-approved consultants and contractors must be engaged for system design and installation. All related service applications are handled via the Benayat electronic platform. EWA has set 300 MW as a net-metering capacity target for 2026, with 50 MW already connected and a further 150 MW in progress as of available reporting.
target_standards: Cabinet Resolution No. 2 of 2017 (Bahrain Net Metering Policy, published in National Gazette 28 December 2017)
EWA approved consultants and contractors requirements
EWA grid technical requirements for distributed renewable energy generation
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-connected inverters are subject to GB/T 19964-2024 for photovoltaic power station grid interconnection and GB/T 37408-2019 inverter technical specifications. Chinese domestic utility interconnection rules do not satisfy EWA material-approval or net-metering requirements in Bahrain.
rows.gap: Inverters must be listed on the EWA-approved materials register before installation in Bahrain net-metering or grid-connected projects. Engage an EWA-approved consultant to confirm the application route via the Benayat platform. China GB/T grid-code compliance does not substitute for EWA approval.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for net-metering and grid-connected solar PV installations in Bahrain; EWA material-approval listing required before installation
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 19964-2024 is a recommended standard; grid connection requires utility approval under China electricity regulations

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EWA material-approval listing is the mandatory gate for inverters in Bahrain net-metering and grid-connected installations. Apply via the EWA Benayat platform with an EWA-approved consultant before supplying inverters to any Bahrain solar project.

source.publisher: Electricity and Water Authority (EWA), Kingdom of Bahrain

rows.requirement: Bahrain Solar PV Market Access and Import Requirements
rows.target_requirement: Bahrain's MOIC oversees general product import controls, while EWA administers the material-approval list for solar PV equipment used in its distribution network. Bahrain does not operate a standalone mandatory product certification scheme for all solar inverters at the import stage equivalent to, for example, China CQC or European CE marking for inverters. The operative requirement for export-to-project is EWA material-approval for grid-connected and net-metering applications. Bahrain targets 255 MW of solar by 2025 from net metering, tenders, and mandatory building requirements, with a national target of 300 MW net-metering capacity by 2026 and broader renewable energy goals under the National Renewable Energy Action Plan.
target_standards: Bahrain National Renewable Energy Action Plan
EWA material-approval process for solar PV equipment
rows.china_equivalent: China solar PV inverter exports may carry China Quality Certification (CQC), China Compulsory Certification (CCC is not applicable to inverters), or third-party IEC test reports. CQC and China-domestic certifications have no standing in Bahrain's EWA material-approval process.
china_standards: CQC (China Quality Certification Centre) voluntary certification
IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 third-party test reports from accredited laboratories
rows.gap: There is no shortcut from China domestic CQC or GB/T certification to Bahrain EWA material-approval. Exporters must submit EWA material-approval applications for each inverter model intended for Bahrain net-metering or grid-connected projects. IEC 62109 accredited-lab reports in English are the expected technical evidence package.

mandatory_status.target: EWA material-approval listing mandatory for grid-connected and net-metering projects; no single mandatory import-level inverter certificate confirmed in public sources
mandatory_status.cn: CQC voluntary; CCC not applicable to inverters; IEC test reports recommended for export

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Bahrain's growing solar market (300 MW net-metering target by 2026) requires EWA material-approval for each inverter model. Chinese domestic certifications do not transfer. Budget time for EWA material-approval application before first project delivery.

source.publisher: Electricity and Water Authority (EWA), Kingdom of Bahrain

rows.requirement: Sustainable Energy Authority (SEA) Net-Metering Policy and Approved Equipment
rows.target_requirement: Bahrain's Sustainable Energy Authority (SEA), formerly referred to as the Sustainable Energy Unit (SEU), provides support and guidance for net-metering applicants and coordinates the national renewable energy policy framework. The Net Metering Policy was approved by Cabinet Resolution No. 2 of 2017. SEA supports parties interested in net-metering and provides guidance on the EWA-administered application process. Only EWA-listed approved equipment, including inverters meeting EWA technical requirements, may be used in net-metering installations. Installers must be EWA-certified contractors. All applications are handled via the Benayat platform.
target_standards: Cabinet Resolution No. 2 of 2017 (Bahrain Net Metering Policy)
EWA approved contractor and consultant requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China's distributed PV net-metering is administered by provincial grid companies under national policy frameworks including the Renewable Energy Law and related NEA regulations. Chinese domestic net-metering procedures and equipment approvals are not recognised in Bahrain's SEA / EWA framework.
rows.gap: Chinese-market inverters have no standing in Bahrain's SEA / EWA net-metering framework. Exporters must obtain EWA material-approval for the specific inverter model, use EWA-certified contractors, and apply through the Benayat platform. Engage an EWA-approved consultant early in the project to confirm equipment eligibility.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for net-metering installations; EWA material-approval and certified contractor requirements are operative conditions for grid connection under the 2017 Net Metering Policy
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for China domestic distributed PV grid connection under NEA regulations; procedures and approvals differ entirely from Bahrain

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Bahrain's net-metering framework requires EWA material-approval for inverters and EWA-certified contractors. China domestic net-metering credentials have no standing in Bahrain. Apply through the Benayat platform with an EWA-approved consultant.

source.publisher: Sustainable Energy Authority (SEA), Kingdom of Bahrain
